sentences.alternateExampleSentence: Most of the amazing tales about the legendary king are entirely apocryphal in nature.

definition: To assess the worth or value of

sentences.quizSentence: An expert was called in to appraise the rare diamond ring.
sentences.exampleSentence: The bank hired a licensed expert to officially appraise the value of the property.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The meticulous jeweler must carefully appraise the diamond necklace before making an offer.

sentences.quizSentence: Police managed to apprehend the bank robber within twenty minutes.
sentences.exampleSentence: The diligent detectives managed to apprehend the elusive thief before he fled the country.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: It took months for law enforcement to finally apprehend the cunning cyber criminal.

sentences.quizSentence: The courageous firefighter earned the approbation of the entire city.
sentences.exampleSentence: The brilliant scientist received universal approbation from her peers after publishing the breakthrough study.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The brilliant scientist received universal approbation for her groundbreaking research on renewable energy.

definition: Based on factors that appear random

sentences.quizSentence: Flipping a coin is an arbitrary way to make a big decision.
sentences.exampleSentence: The tyrannical manager enforced arbitrary rules that frustrated his dedicated and hardworking employees.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: Without clear guidelines, the grading system felt entirely arbitrary to the frustrated students.

sentences.quizSentence: Monkeys are arboreal creatures that spend their lives in the canopy.
sentences.exampleSentence: The rare species of monkey spends its entire life in an arboreal environment.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The dense jungle canopy was teeming with a wide variety of arboreal creatures.

sentences.quizSentence: Historians ascribe the fall of Rome to a multitude of factors.
sentences.exampleSentence: The historian chose to ascribe the fall of the empire to economic instability.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The modest scientist refused to ascribe the brilliant discovery solely to his own genius.

sentences.quizSentence: Her assiduous study habits guaranteed her a perfect score on the exam.
sentences.exampleSentence: The assiduous student spent three hours every single night studying advanced calculus.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: Her assiduous preparation for the bar exam guaranteed a passing score.

definition: To provide evidence or bear witness

sentences.quizSentence: Multiple independent records attest that the treaty was signed in April.
sentences.exampleSentence: Multiple reliable witnesses can firmly attest to the suspect's exact location that evening.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The ancient ruins attest to the remarkable engineering skills of that forgotten civilization.

sentences.quizSentence: The dog whistle produces a sound that is not audible to humans.
sentences.exampleSentence: The sharp crack of the falling tree was clearly audible across the entire valley.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: A sharp, audible crack echoed through the silent forest as the branch snapped.

sentences.quizSentence: The prison cell was stark and austere, containing only a metal cot.
sentences.exampleSentence: The austere monastery lacked modern comforts, focusing strictly on quiet spiritual reflection.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: His austere facial expression intimidated the young recruits during their first training session.

sentences.quizSentence: The con artist used his charm to beguile tourists out of their money.
sentences.exampleSentence: The cunning salesman used smooth flattery to beguile customers into buying useless warranties.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The beautiful music was carefully designed to entirely beguile the wealthy audience members.

definition: Marked by goodness or doing good

sentences.quizSentence: A benevolent donor secretly paid for the sick child's surgery.
sentences.exampleSentence: The benevolent monarch distributed food and warm clothing to the impoverished peasants.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The benevolent teacher spent three extra hours tutoring the struggling student after school.

sentences.quizSentence: The grieving widow felt bereft of comfort after the funeral.
sentences.exampleSentence: The sudden tragedy left the grieving family feeling bereft of hope and joy.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: When her beloved dog passed away, she felt entirely bereft of any remaining joy.

sentences.quizSentence: The scammer managed to bilk elderly residents out of their savings.
sentences.exampleSentence: The clever scammer managed to entirely bilk the elderly woman out of her savings.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The sophisticated con artist managed to bilk elderly investors out of their life savings.

sentences.quizSentence: He tried to blandish the bouncer into letting them skip the line.
sentences.exampleSentence: The eager assistant tried to blandish his demanding boss with constant, insincere compliments.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: She tried to carefully blandish the angry manager into approving her vacation request.

sentences.quizSentence: His bombastic speech lacked any real substance or practical solutions.
sentences.exampleSentence: The politician's bombastic speech lacked real substance despite his dramatic hand gestures.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: Her bombastic attitude alienated the entire team during the crucial software development meeting.

sentences.quizSentence: The sudden rainstorm was a huge boon to the struggling farmers.
sentences.exampleSentence: The sudden heavy rain was an incredible boon to the desperate, drought-stricken farmers.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The sudden discovery of rare minerals was a significant boon to the local economy.

sentences.quizSentence: He rejected his family's bourgeois lifestyle to live as a starving artist.
sentences.exampleSentence: The rebellious artist mocked the bourgeois obsession with manicured lawns and luxury cars.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: His bourgeois lifestyle centered around acquiring expensive clothes and dining at exclusive restaurants.

sentences.quizSentence: A loud cacophony of car horns erupted during the traffic jam.
sentences.exampleSentence: A deafening cacophony of car horns erupted during the severe downtown traffic jam.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The amateur orchestra produced a painful cacophony instead of a harmonious musical performance.

sentences.quizSentence: The devastating earthquake was the worst calamity the nation had ever faced.
sentences.exampleSentence: Failing to secure the fragile cargo resulted in a costly maritime calamity.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The sudden stock market crash proved to be an absolute financial calamity for investors.

sentences.quizSentence: Her capacious handbag held everything from an umbrella to a laptop.
sentences.exampleSentence: The capacious auditorium comfortably seated over three thousand eager students for the graduation ceremony.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: She purchased a capacious leather tote bag to carry her heavy academic textbooks.

sentences.quizSentence: The surrounded army had no choice but to capitulate to the enemy.
sentences.exampleSentence: Refusing to capitulate during the negotiations, the union leaders demanded better working conditions.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The stubborn union refused to capitulate to the greedy demands of the corporate executives.

sentences.quizSentence: The capricious weather shifted from sunny to hailing in minutes.
sentences.exampleSentence: The capricious weather in the mountains can shift from sunny to snowing within minutes.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: His capricious mood swings made it difficult for his friends to plan social outings.

sentences.quizSentence: The protests helped catalyze a major shift in public policy.
sentences.exampleSentence: The shocking documentary helped catalyze a nationwide movement for vital environmental reform.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: Adding a specific chemical enzyme will efficiently catalyze the sluggish biological reaction.

sentences.quizSentence: The sudden fire alarm plunged the crowded theater into total chaos.
sentences.exampleSentence: Absolute chaos erupted in the crowded stadium when the sudden fire alarm unexpectedly sounded.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: Sudden power outages plunged the busy metropolitan train station into unpredictable chaos.

sentences.quizSentence: She gently chided her husband for forgetting to buy milk.
sentences.exampleSentence: The loving mother had to gently chide her son for leaving his toys outside.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The stern librarian had to gently chide the giggling students for making noise.

sentences.quizSentence: This thick volume is a detailed chronicle of the Roman Empire.
sentences.exampleSentence: The detailed journal serves to chronicle the daily hardships of early colonial settlers.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The journalist decided to accurately chronicle the difficult journey of the political refugees.

sentences.quizSentence: The cab driver took a circuitous route to drive up the meter.
sentences.exampleSentence: The lost hikers took a frustratingly circuitous route back to the mountain base.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: His circuitous explanation failed to answer the journalist's direct question about the scandal.

sentences.quizSentence: The guard dogs were kept within a carefully circumscribed area of the yard.
sentences.exampleSentence: The strict prison rules heavily circumscribed the daily activities of the  dangerous inmates.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The strict warden circumscribed the daily activities allowed within the maximum security prison.

sentences.quizSentence: Hackers found a clever way to circumvent the company's new firewall.
sentences.exampleSentence: The clever hacker found a brilliant way to circumvent the new corporate firewall.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: Clever hackers found a novel way to circumvent the corporate firewall security protocols.

sentences.quizSentence: The spies held a clandestine meeting in the back of a dark pub.
sentences.exampleSentence: The secret agents held a clandestine meeting inside the abandoned industrial warehouse tonight.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The secret agents held a clandestine meeting in a dark underground parking garage.

sentences.quizSentence: The butcher used a heavy meat axe to cleave the bone in half.
sentences.exampleSentence: The skilled butcher used a heavy axe to cleave the frozen meat apart.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The dedicated marathoner must entirely cleave to his strict weekly training schedule.

sentences.quizSentence: She put up her car as collateral to secure the bank loan.
sentences.exampleSentence: The bank required the business owner to use his house as loan collateral.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The desperate businessman offered his luxury yacht as collateral for the bank loan.

sentences.quizSentence: The ancient Colossus of Rhodes was a bronze statue honoring the sun god.
sentences.exampleSentence: The ancient bronze colossus guarded the harbor entrance until an earthquake destroyed it.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The new stadium stands as a modern colossus of incredible concrete and steel.

definition: A notice of approval or recognition

sentences.quizSentence: The brave police dog received a special commendation for finding the suspect.
sentences.exampleSentence: The brave firefighter received a formal commendation for saving three trapped apartment residents.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The principal gave a special commendation to the students who organized the charity.

definition: To make an appropriate payment for something

sentences.quizSentence: The airline agreed to compensate the passengers for the delayed flight.
sentences.exampleSentence: The struggling airline promised to financially compensate the passengers for the delayed flight.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The airline must legally compensate passengers for the sudden cancellation of their flight.

definition: Ready to adapt oneself to another’s wishes

sentences.quizSentence: The restaurant kitchen is fully compliant with all state health codes.
sentences.exampleSentence: The compliant child quietly finished his homework without offering any resistance or complaints.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: Restaurants must remain strictly compliant with local health codes to avoid being closed.

sentences.quizSentence: The losing candidate called her rival to concede the tight election.
sentences.exampleSentence: The stubborn politician refused to concede the election despite trailing by thousands of votes.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: He had to concede that his original design for the database was entirely flawed.

sentences.quizSentence: The chef managed to concoct a brilliant sauce out of leftover ingredients.
sentences.exampleSentence: The clever chef managed to concoct a brilliant meal from the leftover ingredients.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The desperate teenager tried to concoct a believable excuse for breaking curfew again.

sentences.quizSentence: Loss of memory is a natural concomitant of advanced old age.
sentences.exampleSentence: The concomitant risks of the radical surgery were carefully explained to the patient.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The rapid economic growth brought a concomitant increase in urban traffic and pollution.

sentences.quizSentence: The two neighboring towns existed in a state of peaceful concord for decades.
sentences.exampleSentence: The two rival nations signed a historic concord to officially end the war.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The historic treaty brought decades of peaceful concord between the two warring nations.

sentences.quizSentence: The school principal refuses to condone bullying of any kind.
sentences.exampleSentence: The university explicitly stated that it does not condone any form of cheating.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The strict university refuses to condone cheating on final exams under any circumstances.

sentences.quizSentence: The bakery displayed a beautiful chocolate confection in the front window.
sentences.exampleSentence: The decadent chocolate confection was decorated with delicate edible gold leaf and raspberries.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The delicate sugar confection was the absolute centerpiece of the beautiful dinner party.

sentences.quizSentence: The dropped cigarette started a conflagration that destroyed the forest.
sentences.exampleSentence: The dry forest provided perfect fuel for the rapidly spreading and deadly conflagration.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: Brave firefighters battled the raging conflagration that threatened to consume the historic library.

sentences.quizSentence: The city was founded right at the confluence of the Ohio and Mississippi rivers.
sentences.exampleSentence: The thriving trading city was built at the confluence of three major rivers.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: A rare confluence of artistic talent and financial backing produced the spectacular film.

sentences.quizSentence: Industrial runoff continues to defile the waters of the local river.
sentences.exampleSentence: The invading army deliberately tried to defile the sacred temple with insulting graffiti.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The invading army sought to defile the sacred temple of the local population.

sentences.quizSentence: The heavy security presence is meant to deter shoplifters from stealing merchandise.
sentences.exampleSentence: The presence of visible security cameras helped to deter shoplifters from targeting stores.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: Harsh winter storms did not deter the brave explorers from reaching the summit.

sentences.quizSentence: The volcano lay dormant for three hundred years before suddenly erupting again.
sentences.exampleSentence: Bears spend the freezing winter months resting inside caves in a dormant state.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The bear retreated to its dark cave to remain dormant throughout winter.

sentences.quizSentence: The effulgent morning sun peeked beautifully over the horizon, lighting up the valley.
sentences.exampleSentence: The bride looked stunning in her effulgent white dress as she walked down.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The effulgent morning sun brilliantly illuminated the dew resting on the green meadow.

sentences.quizSentence: The civil rights leader delivered an eloquent speech that moved everyone to tears.
sentences.exampleSentence: The eloquent speaker moved the audience to tears with her powerful graduation speech.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: Her eloquent and beautifully written essay earned her the top academic prize today.

sentences.quizSentence: He donated half his wealth to charity to expiate his past corporate misconduct.
sentences.exampleSentence: He volunteered at the homeless shelter to expiate his past criminal behavior.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: He volunteered at the homeless shelter to expiate his past criminal transgressions.

definition: To infer unknown values by extending known data

sentences.quizSentence: From three years of scores, the committee extrapolated likely long-term improvement.
sentences.exampleSentence: The scientist can extrapolate the future climate trends based on current rising temperatures.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: It is risky to extrapolate broad societal changes from a single isolated survey.

definition: Meticulous, demanding, having high and often.

sentences.quizSentence: The fastidious chef demands absolute perfection and total cleanliness in his busy kitchen.
sentences.exampleSentence: The fastidious editor demanded flawless grammar and perfect punctuation in every submitted manuscript.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: A fastidious housekeeper will never tolerate even a single speck of visible dust.

sentences.quizSentence: The fecund river valley produces abundant harvests of wheat every year.
sentences.exampleSentence: The fecund soil of the river valley produces abundant crops year after year.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: Her fecund imagination allowed her to write three brilliant fantasy novels in one year.

sentences.quizSentence: The Victorian mansion was decorated with florid, overly complicated gold wallpaper.
sentences.exampleSentence: The architect despised the florid decorations covering the walls of the Victorian mansion.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The embarrassed teenager spoke with a florid face when the teacher called on him.

sentences.quizSentence: Lost hikers had to forage for edible berries and nuts in the dense forest.
sentences.exampleSentence: The desperate survivors had to forage for edible berries in the dense forest.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The desperate raccoons will forage through the neighborhood trash cans every single night.

sentences.quizSentence: The company sold off its valuable real estate to forestall an inevitable, crushing bankruptcy.
sentences.exampleSentence: The central bank raised interest rates to forestall a dangerous spike in inflation.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: He took vitamins daily to forestall the severe symptoms of the winter flu.

definition: Happening by chance, often lucky or fortunate

sentences.quizSentence: The fortuitous discovery of the gold vein transformed the struggling miner.
sentences.exampleSentence: Meeting the influential publisher at the crowded cafe was a fortuitous encounter.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The sudden rainstorm was a fortuitous break for the exhausted marathon runner.

sentences.quizSentence: The neon green sweater was far too garish for a solemn funeral.
sentences.exampleSentence: The wealthy eccentric painted his historical mansion in garish shades of neon pink.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: She wore a garish purple dress that attracted unwanted attention at the formal dinner.

sentences.quizSentence: Surviving the avalanche was a harrowing experience he would never forget.
sentences.exampleSentence: The weary soldiers survived a harrowing journey through the dense, enemy-infested jungle.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The documentary provides a harrowing look at the harsh realities of modern warfare.

sentences.quizSentence: The Roman Empire maintained strict hegemony over the Mediterranean for centuries.
sentences.exampleSentence: The powerful empire maintained political hegemony over the smaller neighboring coastal nations.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The tech giant established total market hegemony by acquiring all its smaller competitors.

definition: Unspeakable, incapable of being expressed through words

sentences.quizSentence: The view of the Grand Canyon at sunrise possesses an ineffable beauty.
sentences.exampleSentence: The breathtaking beauty of the Grand Canyon filled the tourists with ineffable awe.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The stunning sunset over the ocean possessed an ineffable beauty that defied description.

definition: Incapable of being persuaded or placated

sentences.quizSentence: The inexorable march of time slowly turns children into adults.
sentences.exampleSentence: The inexorable march of time eventually turns ancient mountains into flat desert plains.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The inexorable advance of the glacier slowly crushed everything standing in its path.

sentences.quizSentence: The hospital food was so insipid that visitors brought spices from home.
sentences.exampleSentence: The insipid movie lacked any real plot development or interesting character arcs.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The food critic dismissed the chef's new soup as painfully insipid.

sentences.quizSentence: The street magician used quick legerdemain to make the coin vanish into thin air.
sentences.exampleSentence: The magician used clever legerdemain to make the gold coin disappear instantly.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The cunning illusionist used spectacular legerdemain to make the elephant vanish on stage.

definition: Characterized by rich abundance verging on ostentation

sentences.quizSentence: The opulent ballroom featured crystal chandeliers and gold-leaf trim on the walls.
sentences.exampleSentence: The opulent mansion featured gold statues and rare art in every room.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The decadent monarch threw an opulent feast featuring rare exotic meats and wines.

sentences.quizSentence: Her extreme parsimony meant she refused to turn on the heat even in December.
sentences.exampleSentence: The greedy miser was famous throughout the village for his extreme financial parsimony.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The company's parsimony regarding office supplies frustrated the hard working staff members.

sentences.quizSentence: The surface of the placid lake was as smooth and reflective as a mirror.
sentences.exampleSentence: The surface of the lake remained placid throughout the calm summer morning.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: He has a placid temperament that helps him stay calm during stress.

sentences.quizSentence: Her excuse that traffic caused her lateness was entirely plausible during rush hour.
sentences.exampleSentence: The witness provided a plausible explanation for his presence at the scene.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: That is a plausible theory, but it still requires much more evidence.

sentences.quizSentence: The old photograph of his late grandfather brought back a poignant rush of memories.
sentences.exampleSentence: The story had a poignant ending that brought the audience to tears.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: She felt a poignant sense of loss when she left her home.

definition: An aggressive argument against a specific opinion

sentences.quizSentence: The angry journalist published a fiery polemic condemning the city's corrupt mayor.
sentences.exampleSentence: The professor wrote a sharp polemic against the modern education reform bills.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: He is known for publishing a polemic in the local newspaper today.

sentences.quizSentence: The judge is a woman of absolute probity who has never taken a bribe.
sentences.exampleSentence: The judge is known for her high probity and her fair rulings.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: He acted with moral probity even when it was difficult to do.

sentences.quizSentence: His writing style is rather prosaic and lacks any sort of creative imagination.
sentences.exampleSentence: The report was filled with prosaic details that bored the committee members.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: She wanted to escape her prosaic life by moving to the city.

sentences.quizSentence: The opera singer achieved international renown for her breathtaking soprano voice.
sentences.exampleSentence: The author achieved great renown after his first novel became a bestseller.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: The brilliant surgeon achieved global renown for inventing a life-saving cardiovascular operation.

sentences.quizSentence: The invading empire sought to subjugate the local tribes and force them into slavery.
sentences.exampleSentence: The ruthless emperor sought to mercilessly subjugate all rebellious tribes within the territory.
sentences.alternateExampleSentence: They fought to subjugate their fears and finish the dangerous mission tonight.
